skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-doc</name>
    <description>Feishu document read/write operations. Activate when user mentions Feishu docs, cloud docs, or docx links.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-doc/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-drive</name>
    <description>Feishu cloud storage file management. Activate when user mentions cloud space, folders, drive.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-drive/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-perm</name>
    <description>Feishu permission management for documents and files. Activate when user mentions sharing, permissions, collaborators.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-perm/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-wiki</name>
    <description>Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-wiki/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tavily</name>
    <description>Tavily web search, content extraction, and research tools.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/tavily/skills/tavily/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tmux</name>
    <description>Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/tmux/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>video-frames</name>
    <description>Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/video-frames/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

compactionCheckpoints.summary:
## Goal
开发一个完整的 RAG（检索增强生成）系统，集成 FAISS 向量检索和 vLLM 大语言模型，提供 REST API 服务，并创建带有拖拽上传和聊天界面的 Web 应用。

## Constraints & Preferences
- 技术栈：LlamaIndex, FAISS, SentenceTransformers, FastAPI, vLLM
- 模型：qwen3.5-9b (vLLM), all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (嵌入模型)
- 环境：Python 3.12 + uv venv，CPU 模式运行（避免 CUDA 内存问题）
- 数据源路径：`/home/zigaa/.openclaw/workspace/workspace-develop/cleaned_data/samples`
- vLLM 地址：`localhost:11434`（需要认证）
- 输出可复用的配置模板和示例代码
- 定期更新项目进度报告
- 需要支持多种文件格式上传：TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX, DOC

## Progress
### Done
- [x] 环境搭建完成（Python 3.12.13 + uv venv，所有依赖已安装）
- [x] 数据清洗脚本开发完成，支持 PDF/TXT/MD/DOCX/CSV/JSON 格式
- [x] 成功处理 4 个示例文档，生成 11 个清洗后的 chunks
- [x] FAISS 索引成功构建（4 个文档，384 维向量，使用本地模型 `all-MiniLM-L6-v2`）
- [x] FAISS 查询测试通过（查询"什么是 RAG？"，相似度 0.77-0.83）
- [x] vLLM 服务连接成功（`localhost:11434`，模型 qwen3.5-9b，认证配置成功）
- [x] 完整 RAG 服务开发完成（`rag_service_working.py`, `rag_service_final_complete.py`）
- [x] REST API 服务启动成功（端口 8083，健康检查和查询 API 工作正常）
- [x] Web 界面开发完成（`web_rag.html`，支持拖拽上传文档和实时聊天）
- [x] Web 后端服务开发完成（`rag_web_service.py`，支持文档上传/删除/查询）
- [x] 完整使用文档编写完成（`docs/COMPLETE_RAG_GUIDE.md`）
- [x] 完整 RAG 流程测试通过（检索 → 生成 → 返回）
- [x] 创建 PDF 转文本脚本 `pdf_to_text.py`
- [x] 创建 PDF 上传脚本 `upload_pdf.py`
- [x] 开发 RAG Web Service V2 (`rag_web_service_v2.py`)，支持所有格式（TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX, DOC）的自动处理
- [x] 创建新的 Web 界面 V2 (`web_rag_v2.html`)，更新支持格式说明
- [x] 修复了 Web 服务启动问题（缺少 `HTMLResponse` 导入）
- [x] 测试 V2 服务健康状态（`{"status":"healthy","faiss_nodes":1,"vllm":"connected","mode":"full"}`）

### In Progress
- [ ] 解决 Web 服务启动后访问根路径 (`/`) 返回 "Internal Server Error" 的问题

### Blocked
- 无

## Key Decisions
- **放弃 BM25Retriever 和 HuggingFace 方案**：因 `BM25Retriever` 在当前 LlamaIndex 版本中不可用，且 HuggingFace 模型下载和配置问题，转向 FAISS + 本地模型方案。
- **采用 FAISS + vLLM 集成方案**：使用 FAISS 进行向量检索，vLLM 进行生成，支持认证配置和降级模式（仅 FAISS）。
- **使用 FastAPI 构建 REST API**：提供标准化接口，支持异步调用 vLLM。
- **创建 Web 界面**：提供拖拽上传文档和聊天界面，提升用户体验。
- **CPU 模式运行**：避免 CUDA 内存不足问题，使用 `device="cpu"` 运行嵌入模型。
- **创建独立的 PDF 处理脚本**：为解决 PDF 上传失败问题，先创建了 `pdf_to_text.py` 和 `upload_pdf.py` 脚本，作为简化版解决方案。
- **开发 V2 版本支持所有格式**：创建 `rag_web_service_v2.py` 和 `web_rag_v2.html`，内置对 TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX, DOC 文件的自动读取和处理功能。

## Next Steps
1. 修复 Web 服务根路径访问错误，确保 `http://localhost:8083` 能正常显示 V2 界面。
2. 测试 Web 界面的完整流程（上传文档 → 聊天查询）。
3. 优化服务性能和错误处理。
4. 部署到生产环境（可选）。

## Critical Context
- **项目路径**：`/home/zigaa/.openclaw/workspace/workspace-develop/`
- **关键文件**：
  - `rag_service_working.py` - 完整 RAG 服务（已测试）
  - `rag_service_final_complete.py` - 最终版本（带 REST API）
  - `web_rag.html` - Web 界面
  - `rag_web_service.py` - Web 后端服务
  - `docs/COMPLETE_RAG_GUIDE.md` - 完整使用文档
  - `pdf_to_text.py` - PDF 转文本脚本
  - `upload_pdf.py` - PDF 上传脚本
  - `rag_web_service_v2.py` - V2 版 Web 后端服务（支持所有格式）
  - `web_rag_v2.html` - V2 版 Web 界面
- **数据路径**：`/home/zigaa/.openclaw/workspace/workspace-develop/cleaned_data/samples`
- **模型路径**：`/home/zigaa/.openclaw/workspace/workspace-develop/models/sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2`
- **vLLM 配置**：`localhost:11434`，API Key: `sk-123456789abcdef`
- **服务端口**：8083（REST API 和 Web 界面）
- **测试结果**：FAISS 检索相似度 0.77-0.83，vLLM 生成正常，完整 RAG 流程已跑通。V2 服务健康检查通过。
- **错误记录**：
  - 端口冲突：`[Errno 98] error while attempting to bind on address ('0.0.0.0', 8080): address already in use`
  - CUDA 内存不足：`CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 20.00 MiB.`
  - vLLM 认证失败：`{"error":"Unauthorized"}`（已解决）
  - PDF 上传失败：用户报告上传 PDF 文件失败。
  - Web 服务错误：`NameError: name 'HTMLResponse' is not defined`（已修复）
  - 当前错误：访问 `http://localhost:8083/` 返回 "Internal Server Error"

## Decisions
- 采用 FAISS 方案作为当前可行方案。
- 集成 vLLM 并成功配置认证。
- 创建 Web 界面提供拖拽上传和聊天体验。
- 使用 CPU 模式避免 GPU 内存问题。
- 为解决 PDF 上传问题，先创建独立的 PDF 处理脚本。
- 开发 V2 版本，内置多格式文件支持，放弃依赖外部 Markdown 解析工具。

## Open TODOs
- 修复 Web 服务根路径访问错误。
- 完善 Web 界面的错误提示和用户体验。
- 性能优化和监控。

## Constraints/Rules
- 使用 LlamaIndex 技术栈。
- 环境限制：Python 3.12，无 OpenAI API 密钥。
- 输出路径：`/home/zigaa/.openclaw/workspace/workspace-develop`。
- 定期更新云文档：`https://feishu.cn/docx/O7eBdIJSdo8LQlxIEaVcAaa3n2f`。

## Pending user asks
- 大哥询问 RAG 项目状态（"去看看 rag 项目怎么样了"）。
- 大哥确认开始执行（"开始"）。
- 大哥询问是否需要继续等待模型下载（"需要我继续等待模型下载，还是有其他指示？"）。
- 大哥报告 PDF 上传失败（"上传失败：OpenClaw 2026.4.9 完整使用手册（命令+官方文档整合版）-0413.pdf"）。

## Exact identifiers
- 用户 ID：`ou_5e976f1bebfd947610b5550fa70ef381`
- 消息 ID：`om_x100b5132bf21d0a0c24a909399b69a4`, `om_x100b513e4fba5cb0c49a6bf713f77b8`, `om_x100b513e4a1988a8c36fcabe2359cc3`, `om_x100b513e48cbfcb4c4c47e6177a1a09`, `om_x100b513e5b9cecbcc21cd2f045ec20c`, `om_x100b513ee67624b8c10943356a48909`, `om_x100b513effefa8a0c3b18a54bd37aed`, `om_x100b513efc1c88acc3ce08293b4f605`, `om_x100b513e84a89c5cc3b7c5ffd89766d`, `om_x100b513ebecfa080c24d0618f85a892`, `om_x100b513f571604acc32db8f166cfd9f`, `om_x100b513f7655e488c211b9585247d22`

- (none)

2. 检查相关服务（如 vLLM: localhost:11434）和端口（t:8083）的状态。

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-doc</name>
    <description>Feishu document read/write operations. Activate when user mentions Feishu docs, cloud docs, or docx links.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-doc/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-drive</name>
    <description>Feishu cloud storage file management. Activate when user mentions cloud space, folders, drive.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-drive/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-perm</name>
    <description>Feishu permission management for documents and files. Activate when user mentions sharing, permissions, collaborators.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-perm/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>feishu-wiki</name>
    <description>Feishu knowledge base navigation. Activate when user mentions knowledge base, wiki, or wiki links.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-wiki/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tavily</name>
    <description>Tavily web search, content extraction, and research tools.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/tavily/skills/tavily/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tmux</name>
    <description>Remote-control tmux sessions for interactive CLIs by sending keystrokes and scraping pane output.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/tmux/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>video-frames</name>
    <description>Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/video-frames/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>
